title: Refactor elo_ladder onto the JAX agent (drop torch arena/play)
description: elo_ladder.py imports MCTSPlayer/Player/RandomPlayer/play_match from torch arena and load_checkpoint from torch play. Rewire it onto the JAX stack: load checkpoints via jaxzero.train.load_checkpoint, wrap with the JaxMCTSAgent already in alphazero/c4_certify.py, and add a torch-free play_match that plays two agents on alphazero.games.connectfour (Python loop over initial_state/apply_move/is_terminal/winner using agent.move). Keep the Elo-fitting math. Must import no torch. Blocks Phase 4 (alphago-bdi) since bdi deletes arena/play. Epic alphago-6ud. See docs/JAX_C4_MIGRATION.md.

title: Phase 4: remove PyTorch; pure-JAX repo
description: Final big-bang removal once JAX C4 trains + certifies + runs on GPU. Delete torch modules (network.py, train.py, arena.py, play.py, benchmark.py) and torch from modal_app.py; remove superseded mcts.py/selfplay.py if unused; drop torch from pyproject deps; add jax/pgx/mctx/flax/optax; update __init__ + tests; repo lands torch-free. No backwards compat. RULE 1: explicit deletion approval before rm. Epic alphago-6ud; also blocked by Phase 2 (alphago-h7q) and Phase 3 (alphago-rc8). See docs/JAX_C4_MIGRATION.md.

title: Phase 3: JAX Modal GPU training entrypoint + persisted checkpoints
description: New JAX Modal app: jax[cuda12] image, GPU, runs the Phase-1 training loop on GPU, persists checkpoints to the existing alphazero-checkpoints Volume (reuse the /checkpoints/run_tag/game/ scheme), logs to the per-game wandb project. Replaces the torch modal_app.py training path. Epic alphago-6ud; blocked by Phase 1 (alphago-vlr). See docs/JAX_C4_MIGRATION.md.
close_reason: JAX Modal GPU training entrypoint (jax[cuda12], checkpoint Volume persistence, wandb); 4 fake-modal tests + ruff + ubs green; merged in #36

title: Phase 2: framework-agnostic certify bridge (JAX agent vs c4_solver)
description: Refactor alphazero/c4_certify.py to a framework-agnostic Agent protocol (board to move/value), decoupled from torch MCTSPlayer/load_checkpoint. Add adapter: pgx connect_four board state to/from c4_solver ConnectFourState. JAX agent = Phase-1 net + mctx (a few sims) implements the protocol. Certify a trained checkpoint vs c4_solver: blunder-rate, policy-match, value-MAE, solved verdict. Keep c4_solver.py unchanged. Tests. Epic alphago-6ud; blocked by Phase 1 (alphago-vlr). See docs/JAX_C4_MIGRATION.md.
close_reason: Framework-agnostic certify bridge: adapter obs/legal/terminated match pgx (0/949), agent move faithful (0/33 vs pgx search), c4_solver unchanged, no torch; 6 tests + gate green; merged in #37

title: Phase 1: JAX C4 training pipeline (flax nnx + pgx + mctx + optax)
description: New package jaxzero/: (1) Flax NNX AlphaZero residual net (configurable channels/num_res_blocks; C4 obs from pgx connect_four). (2) mctx Gumbel self-play producing training data (obs, policy target = action_weights, value target = discounted returns, mask) via the lax.scan structure in experiments/jax_c4_spike/bench.py. (3) optax (adam) training loop: policy cross-entropy + value MSE; buffer-free (train on fresh per-iteration self-play). (4) checkpoint save/load (flax params; orbax or msgpack). (5) a train CLI + a fast hermetic smoke test (few iters, tiny net) + determinism (seeded). No torch. See docs/JAX_C4_MIGRATION.md. Code-only commit (no .beads). Open a PR.
close_reason: JAX C4 training pipeline; trains end-to-end (loss 2.94->2.59), checkpoint reloads, 7 tests + ruff + ubs green, torch-free; merged in #35

title: Migrate to pure-JAX AlphaZero, Connect Four first
description: Replace PyTorch with a JAX stack (pgx connect_four + mctx Gumbel AlphaZero + Flax NNX net + optax). No backwards compatibility / no dual stack. C4 is the pilot: small + has an exact oracle (c4_solver) to validate correctness. Goal: drive C4 to solved (blunder-rate 0 on c4_certify) on GPU. Decisions: big-bang (no parallel torch baseline kept), Flax NNX, pgx env, buffer-free start, mctx-based certify. Spec in docs/JAX_C4_MIGRATION.md. Keep framework-agnostic c4_solver.py + elo_ladder.py; refactor c4_certify to an agent protocol; remove the 6 torch files in the final phase.

title: Persist Modal-trained checkpoints to a Volume
description: modal_app.py trained with checkpoint_path=None, discarding every cloud run's net on container teardown (campaign 7jlfmjgu's net was lost, uncertifiable). Mount a persistent Modal Volume and save final + optional periodic checkpoints under /checkpoints/<run_tag>/<game>/.
close_reason: Modal Volume checkpoint persistence (final + optional periodic); merged in #32

title: C4 solved-ness certification harness
description: New module alphazero/c4_certify.py (do NOT edit arena.py or c4_solver.py): given a trained C4 net checkpoint, sample a diverse position set (random self-play lines plus systematic opening lines), and for each solver-evaluable position compare the agent MCTS move + value to the solver optimal move + value. Report policy-match percent, blunder-rate (agent picks a move that worsens the game-theoretic win/loss/draw outcome), value-MAE, and a single solved verdict (blunder-rate == 0 over the sample). Use the existing solve() API from c4_solver, MCTSPlayer + play_match from arena, and the Game interface. Deterministic-seeded, configurable sample size, with a CLI entrypoint. Build against the CURRENT solve() API; opening certification activates automatically once the solver extension lands. File-scope: alphazero/c4_certify.py + tests/test_c4_certify.py (+ pyproject script entry).
close_reason: C4 certification harness; blunder logic verified (negamax re-solve), 3 tests pass; merged in #26

title: Extend C4 solver to certify play from the opening
description: alphazero/c4_solver.py currently solves only with a node budget (250k) and <=16 empty cells, so it certifies endgames but not the opening (where C4's proven first-player win lives). Make solve() return the exact game-theoretic value + optimal moves from the empty board and any position, using standard strong-solver techniques (full-window search, much larger or removed node budget, iterative deepening, strong move ordering, transposition table - much already exists). KEEP the solve() signature stable (value, optimal_moves) so the certification harness can rely on it. Add tests on known positions. File-scope: alphazero/c4_solver.py + tests/test_c4_solver.py only.
close_reason: C4 opening book (proven values) + bounded search; empty->( 1,[3]), after-center->-1 all-optimal, deep->skip; verified; merged in #28

title: Solve Connect Four
description: Drive a trained AlphaZero agent to optimal (solved) Connect Four, the project's first non-trivial solved game. C4 is the sweet spot: game-theoretically solved (first-player win) AND it already has an exact oracle. Plan: (A) extend the C4 solver to certify play from the OPENING, not just the endgame (current solver is budgeted to <=16 empty cells / 250k nodes); (B) a solved-ness certification harness that reports blunder-rate, policy-match, value-MAE across a diverse position set incl. opening lines, with a single solved verdict; (C) the Modal training campaign tracking those metrics until blunder-rate hits 0. Bar: agent never makes a game-theoretically losing/value-degrading move on any solver-certifiable position.

title: Checkpoint Elo ladder: absolute strength eval for Go/Gomoku
description: Limiting factor (2026-05-27 reflection): gating-Elo is self-referential (candidate vs a frozen best) and sits flat at 0 for go because the candidate rarely clears the promotion threshold; go/gomoku have no absolute strength anchor (no tractable perfect player, no solver beyond C4, negamax ladder capped at depth-1 for tractability). So we cannot tell whether a longer training run is learning, plateaued, or diverging, which makes scaling compute blind. Build an absolute anchored strength signal: (1) Periodic checkpointing in train_agent: save the net every N iterations (e.g. checkpoints/<game>/iter_NNNN.pt), not just the final/best net. (2) A game-agnostic checkpoint Elo ladder evaluator (MCTSPlayer + play_match): anchor a fixed reference at Elo 0 (the initial or random-policy checkpoint), play seeded games among checkpoints (anchored-sequential vs reference plus neighbors for O(n) cost, or round-robin for accuracy), and fit Elo from pairwise win/draw/loss results (iterative or logistic update). Log the per-checkpoint anchored Elo curve to wandb. Result: a readable learning curve (checkpoint index vs anchored Elo) that shows if a long Go run is improving, the prerequisite for committing real training compute. Complements (does not replace) the per-iteration gating-Elo. Keep it deterministic-seeded and configurable (games per pairing, anchor choice, sequential vs round-robin).
close_reason: Checkpoint Elo ladder + periodic checkpointing; Elo-fit properties verified (anchor=0, monotone, deterministic); merged in #25

title: Restore Modal training entrypoint (modal_app.py emptied by #7)
description: PR #7 ('Slim Modal image to CPU torch + game-aware wandb project') deleted modal_app.py (375 lines) and tests/test_modal_app.py (348 lines) instead of editing them; both are 0 bytes in main. 'modal run modal_app.py' is broken. #7's titled improvements (CPU torch image, game-aware wandb project) never shipped — runs logged to hardcoded WANDB_PROJECT='alphazero-tictactoe'. Restore the matched pair from 833d1fd~1 and apply #7's intent properly (CPU image + _default_wandb_project).
close_reason: Restored Modal entrypoint + #7 intent; merged in #12

title: Exact Connect Four solver (bitboard negamax+alpha-beta+TT)
description: New file alphazero/c4_solver.py: exact C4 oracle (eval-only, C4-specific). Bitboard negamax + alpha-beta + transposition table + center move-ordering (Pons approach). API solve(state)->(value in {-1,0,1}, optimal_moves). Node/time budget guard. Thorough correctness tests on known positions. ONLY new files.
close_reason: Exact solver verified independently: value + complete optimal_moves match brute force on 30 positions

title: Self-play improvement eval: gating + Elo + negamax ladder
description: Wire strength evaluation into arena.train_agent so improvement is measurable: (1) gating + Elo (snapshot candidate every K iters, play candidate-vs-best match, promote on >=55%, update Elo, generate self-play from best); (2) negamax-depth ladder + random win-rates per eval interval; log all to wandb (eval/elo, eval/gating_winrate, eval/promoted, eval/ladder_*). CLI flags + fast hermetic tests. Edit arena.py + test_arena.py only.
close_reason: Eval works; cold-start fixed — Elo climbs 0->45, gating promotes, d4 ladder informative (wandb 1skg9znt)

title: JAX C4 self-play GPU throughput benchmark on Modal
description: Port experiments/jax_c4_spike/bench.py to a Modal GPU container (jax[cuda12] + pgx + mctx) to measure self-play throughput on a GPU and compare to the CPU parity result (JAX 2.3 / PyTorch 2.08 games/s on 8 cores). This is the architecture that actually uses a GPU (on-device vmap batched self-play), so it is the real test of whether the GPU/JAX bet pays off for scaling. Deliverable: GPU games/sec at large batch + a go/no-go.
close_reason: JAX GPU benchmark on Modal (A10G): batch128 19.9, batch512 39.6, batch2048 50.0 games/s vs 2.08 PyTorch-CPU (~9-24x); merged in #33

title: JAX+pgx+mctx Connect Four self-play spike (throughput benchmark)
description: Research spike (separate, non-disruptive): build a minimal JAX self-play loop for Connect Four using pgx (connect_four env) + mctx (Gumbel AlphaZero) + a small Flax/Optax net, and benchmark self-play throughput (games/sec, env-steps/sec) head-to-head against the current PyTorch campaign (run 7jlfmjgu logs self_play_games_per_sec). Goal: a real apples-to-apples speed number to decide whether a JAX 'scale Go' track is worth it. Keep it isolated in experiments/ with its own deps (do NOT add jax/pgx/mctx to the main project). Deliverable: throughput comparison + a go/no-go writeup. Pedagogical PyTorch repo stays the reference implementation.
close_reason: JAX+pgx+mctx C4 throughput spike; CPU parity (2.3 vs 2.08 games/s); merged in #31

title: Incremental/bitboard win-check for Connect Four
description: Profiling (pane 1826 benchmark) shows alphazero/games/connectfour.py winner() is ~35 percent of self-play non-inference time: a full-board O(rows*cols*dirs) scan called ~23k times, once per MCTS node via is_terminal. Replace it with a fast bitboard win detection that preserves EXACT winner() semantics (returns +1, -1, 0, or None identically). Recommended approach: build a per-player bitboard from the board and check four-in-a-row via shift operations (mirror the technique in alphazero/c4_solver.py _has_won), keeping the winner(state) signature and behavior identical and NOT changing the ConnectFourState shape (so direct constructions in tests do not break). Add a parity test asserting the fast winner matches the previous scan across many random and terminal positions. This speeds up ALL Connect Four self-play, MCTS, and eval, not just the campaign. File scope: alphazero/games/connectfour.py and tests/test_connectfour.py only.
close_reason: Bitboard winner() for Connect Four; parity verified (8826 positions, 0 mismatches vs scan); merged in #29

title: C4 training campaign to zero blunders
description: Run the real C4 training campaign on Modal (isolated container, parallel self-play), tracking the solver-anchor + certification metrics as the headline curve, iterating network size / sims / exploration until blunder-rate hits 0 on the certification harness. Blocked by the solver extension and certification harness. Orchestrated (launched + monitored), not a code task.

title: Parallelize self-play across CPU cores + set Modal cpu=
description: Self-play games run sequentially in train_agent (alphazero/arena.py: 'for _ in range(self_play_games_per_iteration): play_game(...)'), so games/sec is low and CPU-bound on NN inference (go/gomoku ~0.3 games/sec isolated on Modal). The N games per iteration are independent (each gets its own game_seed) so they are embarrassingly parallel. Parallelize self-play across CPU cores (process pool; each worker rebuilds the net from state_dict, runs its share of games with per-game seeds, returns examples). Also set cpu= on the modal_app @app.function (currently unset -> small default). Keep determinism, preserve interfaces, make it configurable + fall back to sequential, add tests. No GPU needed.
close_reason: Parallel self-play + Modal cpu= merged in #22

title: Make eval game-agnostic + extend Modal to all games
description: arena.main and modal_app ran PerfectPlayer (full minimax) for any non-C4 game, which hangs on gomoku/go. Gate PerfectPlayer to tictactoe; benchmark gomoku/go vs random (gating+negamax ladder already run inside train_agent). Extend modal_app to construct any game via the registry, add gomoku/go training defaults with a shallow ladder (deep negamax intractable on big boards). Per-game wandb projects already via _wandb_project_for_game.
close_reason: Game-agnostic eval + Modal-all-games merged in #20

title: Add Go (7x7) game with ko, suicide, area scoring
description: Marquee game: pass action (action_size=size^2+1), capture via flood-fill, no-suicide rule, simple single-point ko, two-pass termination, Tromp-Taylor area scoring with half-integer komi (decisive). Configurable size (default 7) + komi. Registered via central registry. Tests cover captures, suicide vs capturing-suicide, ko ban + clear, scoring/komi.
close_reason: Go merged in #16

title: Add Gomoku (five-in-a-row) game
description: New game module exercising the game-agnostic pipeline: placement game (no gravity), configurable board size/connect (default 9x9 connect-5), registered via the central registry. Adds tests for win directions, connect-5 non-win at 4, tiny-board draw, canonical encoding, validation.
close_reason: Gomoku merged in #15

title: Centralize game registry (single GAMES table)
description: Game-name->Game lookup was duplicated in benchmark.py, play.py, arena.py (each with its own _game_from_name + hardcoded argparse choices). Centralize into alphazero/games/__init__.py (GAMES, game_from_name, GAME_CHOICES) so adding a game is one entry. Unblocks Gomoku/Go without 4-file edits.
close_reason: Central registry merged in #13

title: Wire batched MCTS into training self-play (currently sequential)
description: Batched/leaf-parallel MCTS (#9, 2.76-4.18x in benchmark) is only reachable via benchmark.py --mcts-batch-size. Training self-play builds self_play_mcts_cfg={num_simulations,dirichlet_eps} (arena.py:974), so MCTS uses default batch_size=1 (mcts.py:70) -> sequential. wandb config for the 5 C4 runs confirms no batch key. Add a self-play MCTS batch-size knob threaded into self_play_mcts_cfg + the Modal entrypoint so the speedup applies to real training (~0.4 -> ~1.1-1.7 self-play games/sec expected).
close_reason: Batched MCTS wired into training self-play; merged in #17

title: Add Modal smoke test to CI
description: CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) has no modal coverage, which is why #7 silently deleting modal_app.py went unnoticed. Add a lightweight check (import/app-definition assertion, or a 1-iteration modal run smoke) so a broken or missing Modal entrypoint fails CI.
close_reason: wontfix: maintainer does not want CI for this repo

title: Add CI (GitHub Actions: uv sync + pytest + ubs on PRs)
description: No CI exists; tests and ubs are run by hand. Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs uv sync --extra dev, uv run pytest, and ubs on PRs/pushes to catch regressions. NOTE: get explicit approval for any step that downloads+executes binaries (per AGENTS.md); pin action versions to SHAs.
close_reason: Merged to main (#9/#10)

title: Implement batched / leaf-parallel MCTS
description: MCTS currently does sequential single-position network inference, which is the throughput bottleneck and the reason GPU does not help. Implement batched/leaf-parallel MCTS (collect leaves, evaluate the network in a batch) and/or vectorized parallel self-play. Measured by the benchmark bead alphago-bqz; this implements the speedup.
close_reason: Merged to main (#9/#10)

title: Diagnose/fix Agent Mail MCP for ntm workers
description: Every ntm worker this session failed to start mcp_agent_mail (HTTP handshake to 127.0.0.1 failed), so workers had no lease-based file-reservation coordination (we worked around it with disjoint files + central commits). Diagnose and fix so multi-agent file leasing works -- important before scaling parallel worktree rounds.
close_reason: Merged to main (#9/#10)

title: Slim Modal image to CPU-only torch
description: The Modal image pip_installs default torch, pulling full CUDA + nvidia-* wheels even though training runs on CPU (multi-GB image, slow first build). Use the CPU-only torch wheel/index for the default image; keep an optional GPU image for --gpu. Touches modal_app.py image build.
close_reason: Merged to main

title: Make wandb project name game-aware (not hardcoded alphazero-tictactoe)
description: WANDB_PROJECT is hardcoded to alphazero-tictactoe, so Connect Four runs log into the tic-tac-toe project. Make it game-aware (per-game default project or derive from --game), keep --wandb-project override. Touches arena.py + modal_app.py.
close_reason: Merged to main

title: Benchmark + profile training to find bottlenecks and measure speedups
description: Add a benchmarking/profiling harness to identify where training time goes and quantify speedup options. Scope: (1) per-component wall-clock breakdown per iteration -- self-play/MCTS vs network inference vs train step vs eval (gating/ladder/solver) -- exposed as metrics (and optionally to wandb). (2) A profiling mode (cProfile and/or torch.profiler; py-spy notes) to find hotspots. (3) A standalone benchmark script (e.g. alphazero/benchmark.py or scripts/) that runs a fixed workload and reports the breakdown + nodes/sec, games/sec, net-evals/sec. (4) Identify the dominant cost (expected: sequential single-position MCTS net inference) and measure candidate speedups: batched/leaf-parallel MCTS, vectorized/parallel self-play, torch eval()/no_grad/inference_mode, intra-op thread settings, float32 vs half, optional GPU batching. (5) Document findings (a short docs note or README section) and which levers help. INFRA/eval-only -- must not change learning behavior or results. Good first step before scaling to Connect Four-at-strength / larger games.
close_reason: Benchmark harness merged (#6); confirmed dominant cost = sequential MCTS net inference (~68-74%), motivates batched-MCTS bead alphago-7ck

title: Solver-backed C4 eval anchor (value-MAE, policy-match, blunder rate)
description: Using c4_solver, add eval-only metrics in arena C4 path: value-MAE (net value vs true value), policy-match (net/MCTS argmax in solver optimal set), blunder rate (agent move worsens solver-optimal outcome), on sampled reachable mid-game positions; log eval/c4_value_mae, eval/c4_policy_match, eval/c4_blunder_rate to wandb. Eval-only; no training change.
close_reason: Verified: optimal player ->policy_match=1.0/blunder=0.0, blunderer ->1.0/0.0; metrics correct

title: Thread gating/Elo/ladder eval params through Modal
description: modal_app.py already passes wandb_run to train_agent (which logs eval/elo, gating, ladder internally), so Modal runs log them with defaults. Expose the params (gating_interval, gating_games, gating_threshold, eval_interval, ladder_games, ladder_depths) on train_remote + the local_entrypoint as CLI flags and pass them into train_agent so cloud runs are tunable; keep tic-tac-toe path. Update test_modal_app.py.
close_reason: Verified: Modal cloud run logs eval/elo+gating+ladder; Elo 0->6->10 (wandb 5ogsuuzl)

title: Human-vs-agent play CLI (new file)
description: Add alphazero/play.py: a CLI to play a human vs a trained MCTSPlayer for tictactoe or connectfour (--game), printing the board each move. New file + tests/test_play.py only; do NOT edit other modules.
close_reason: Done: play CLI + tests

title: Negamax/alpha-beta baseline opponent (new file)
description: Add alphazero/baselines.py with a NegamaxPlayer (depth-limited alpha-beta + simple heuristic) implementing the Player protocol, game-agnostic, plus tests/test_baselines.py. ONLY new files; do NOT edit arena.py/game.py/etc.
close_reason: Done: negamax baseline + tests

title: Train Connect Four on Modal + verify
description: Train C4 on Modal; verify tactical correctness (take-win/block-loss), high win% vs random, self-improvement vs checkpoint; then push + PR.
close_reason: Verified: C4 self-play tactics win/block=1.0, 40-0-0 vs random (wandb 5c0u17rd)

title: Modal --game support for Connect Four
description: Add --game to modal_app.py train_remote + entrypoint so Connect Four trains on Modal with heavier defaults; keep tic-tac-toe path; local unchanged.
close_reason: Done: modal_app.py --game connectfour + tests

title: Optimize parallel self-play: persistent pool + round-robin chunking
description: Parallel self-play (alphago-wrp, #22) gives ~2.9x on gomoku self-play but only ~1.7x on go, ~1.8x end-to-end. Two limits: (1) the process pool is created and torn down every iteration (each iteration spawns N torch processes ~1-2s each) -> reuse a persistent pool across iterations to amortize spawn cost. (2) games are split into contiguous chunks, so with few games over many workers, go's high game-length variance causes load imbalance (one long game stalls a worker while others idle) -> use round-robin/dynamic dispatch (e.g. imap_unordered over per-game tasks) so all workers stay busy. Baseline: gomoku 0.22->0.64 self-play games/sec (1->8 workers), go 0.22->0.37. Keep determinism (parallel == sequential examples).
close_reason: Persistent pool + per-game dynamic dispatch; determinism verified across iterations; merged in #23

title: Multi-seed Connect Four robustness run
description: We verified tic-tac-toe across 5 seeds but Connect Four only single-seed. Run C4 training across multiple seeds (on Modal) and report tactics/ladder/Elo + solver-anchor metrics per seed to show the results are not seed-luck.
close_reason: Multi-seed C4 robustness documented; merged in #11

title: Fix uv optional-extra ergonomics (avoid env thrash)
description: uv run without the right --extra repeatedly dropped modal/dev from the synced .venv this session (modal not found / pytest not found failures). Add a make target or scripts (e.g. make test, make modal) or a uv config so the correct extras are always present and uv run does not thrash the shared env.
close_reason: Merged to main

title: Trim/gitignore bd scaffolding from the public repo
description: bd init committed .beads scaffolding into the public repo: interactions.jsonl churns on every bd op (perpetual git noise), and hooks/ (a generic ruff/clippy pre-commit) + README.md are tracked. Gitignore interactions.jsonl + hooks/ (and consider README) so only issues.jsonl/config.yaml/metadata.json are tracked; git rm --cached the rest.
close_reason: Merged to main

title: README: Connect Four section
description: Add a Connect Four section to README.md (game, --game flag, eval approach: tactical+random, Modal usage). ONLY edit README.md.
close_reason: Done: README Connect Four section
